# Load the quranic_verses.json and inspect structure, and search for the phrase.
import json, os, sys, re
p = '/mnt/data/file-HHumqWkCbpLvx5yFcuNaEH-quranic_verses.json'
with open(p, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    data = json.load(f)
len(data), data[0:3]

target = "وَالَّذِينَ يَبْخَلُونَ وَيَأْمُرُونَ النَّاسَ بِالْبُخْلِ وَيَكْتُمُونَ مَا آتَاهُمُ اللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِ"
matches = [d for d in data if target in d['ayah_text']]
len(matches), matches[:5]

import re, unicodedata
arabic_diacritics = re.compile(r'[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u0670\u06D6-\u06ED]')
tatweel_re = re.compile(r'ـ+')
punct_re = re.compile(r'[\u0600-\u06FF\u0750-\u077F\u08A0-\u08FF\w]+', flags=re.UNICODE)

def normalize_ar(text):
    # remove tatweel and diacritics
    t = text
    t = tatweel_re.sub('', t)
    t = arabic_diacritics.sub('', t)
    # unify alef variants
    t = t.replace('أ','ا').replace('إ','ا').replace('آ','ا')
    # unify hamza on wae etc maybe keep alef?
    t = t.replace('ؤ','و').replace('ئ','ي')  # sometimes used in normalization
    t = t.replace('ى','ي').replace('ة','ه')
    # remove punctuation and non letters/digits
    # keep arabic letters and numbers and space
    t = re.sub(r'[^\u0621-\u064A0-9\s]', '', t)
    # collapse spaces and lowercase not needed for Arabic
    t = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', t).strip()
    return t

norm_target = normalize_ar(target)
norm_target[:120], len(norm_target)

# Build a normalized index for ayah_texts
hits = []
for d in data:
    n = normalize_ar(d['ayah_text'])
    if norm_target in n:
        hits.append(d)
len(hits), hits[:3]

# find surah 'النساء' entries
surah4 = [d for d in data if d['surah_name'] == 'النساء']
len(surah4), surah4[0:10]

# find ayah_id 37 in surah 4
verse37 = [d for d in surah4 if d['ayah_id']==37]
verse37

# retrieve candidate ayah_text for 4:37
candidate = verse37[0]['ayah_text']
candidate, len(candidate)

def token_f1(a,b):
    atoks = a.split()
    btoks = b.split()
    atset = set(atoks)
    btset = set(btoks)
    common = atset & btset
    # But token F1 should consider multiplicity; use Counters
from collections import Counter
def token_f1_counts(a,b):
    ca = Counter(a.split())
    cb = Counter(b.split())
    common = sum(min(ca[t], cb[t]) for t in (set(ca.keys())|set(cb.keys())))
    precision = common / sum(ca.values()) if sum(ca.values())>0 else 0
    recall = common / sum(cb.values()) if sum(cb.values())>0 else 0
    if precision+recall==0:
        return 0.0
    f1 = 2*precision*recall/(precision+recall)
    return f1, precision, recall, ca, cb

norm_candidate = normalize_ar(candidate)
norm_user = normalize_ar("وَالَّذِينَ يَبْخَلُونَ وَيَأْمُرُونَ النَّاسَ بِالْبُخْلِ وَيَكْتُمُونَ مَا آتَاهُمُ اللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِ")
norm_candidate, norm_user, token_f1_counts(norm_candidate, norm_user)

# compute longest common subsequence length of characters
def lcs_length(a,b):
    n,m = len(a), len(b)
    # DP optimized using 2 rows
    dp_prev = [0]*(m+1)
    for i in range(1,n+1):
        dp = [0]*(m+1)
        ai = a[i-1]
        for j in range(1,m+1):
            if ai == b[j-1]:
                dp[j] = dp_prev[j-1] + 1
            else:
                dp[j] = max(dp_prev[j], dp[j-1])
        dp_prev = dp
    return dp_prev[m]

lcs_len = lcs_length(norm_candidate, norm_user)
len_candidate = len(norm_candidate)
len_user = len(norm_user)
lcs_len, len_candidate, len_user, lcs_len/max(len_candidate,len_user)

import difflib
s = difflib.SequenceMatcher(None, norm_user, norm_candidate)
s.ratio()

# compute Levenshtein distance
def levenshtein(a,b):
    n,m = len(a), len(b)
    dp = list(range(m+1))
    for i,ch in enumerate(a,1):
        prev = i
        new = [i] + [0]*m
        for j,ch2 in enumerate(b,1):
            cost = 0 if ch==ch2 else 1
            new[j] = min(dp[j]+1, new[j-1]+1, dp[j-1]+cost)
        dp = new
    return dp[m]

lev = levenshtein(norm_user, norm_candidate)
lev, len(norm_user), len(norm_candidate), 1 - lev/max(len(norm_user), len(norm_candidate))

def normalize_strip_waw(text):
    t = normalize_ar(text)
    tokens = t.split()
    tokens2 = [tok[1:] if tok.startswith('و') and len(tok)>1 else tok for tok in tokens]
    return ' '.join(tokens2)

ns_candidate = normalize_strip_waw(candidate)
ns_user = normalize_strip_waw("وَالَّذِينَ يَبْخَلُونَ وَيَأْمُرُونَ النَّاسَ بِالْبُخْلِ وَيَكْتُمُونَ مَا آتَاهُمُ اللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِ")
ns_candidate, ns_user, longest_common_token_substring(ns_candidate, ns_user)/len(ns_user.split())
